title: Tender Smoky Saudi Arabian Grape Leaves Stew
description: A savory and velvety Saudi Arabian dish featuring grape leaves, perfect for a lazy Sunday, this stew is a satvik-friendly delight with a smoky twist.
instructions: Rinse the grape leaves and remove the stems, then set them aside.
Heat the olive oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional minute, until fragrant.
Add the smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add the vegetable broth, lemon juice, and chopped parsley, and stir to combine.
Add the grape leaves to the pot, folding them in half to fit if necessary.
Bring the stew to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 30 minutes.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
